title: coreopsis buchii
intro: Coreopsis buchii, also known as Tickseed, is a flowering plant native to the Americas, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Coreopsis buchii include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal infusions to support overall well-being.
